linked_context.page_description: Contribute to rusackas/localtalk development by creating an account on GitHub.
linked_context.page_excerpt:
A minimal macOS menubar app for local, private speech-to-text dictation.

Hold a key, speak, release — your words appear wherever the cursor is. No cloud, no subscription, no data leaving your machine.

The Whisper small.en model (~150 MB) downloads automatically on first launch and is cached locally.

hacker_news.title: Show HN: Canipls – see caniuse.com global support percentages as you type
hacker_news.author: taylorplewe
hacker_news.summary: Language server that shows caniuse support percentages right in your editor - taylorplewe/canipls

linked_context.page_title: GitHub - taylorplewe/canipls: Language server that shows caniuse support percentages right in your editor
linked_context.page_description: Language server that shows caniuse support percentages right in your editor - taylorplewe/canipls
linked_context.page_excerpt:
A language server that shows caniuse.com support percentages for web features, right in your editor.

Implements the following features of Microsoft's Language Server Protocol :

While global support percentages from canipls rarely match 1:1 those of caniuse.com's, they are never more than ~1% off , and rarely more than about 0.1% off. canipls data is refreshed once a day, but caniuse.com does not divulge all of its data sources.

hacker_news.content_context:
This is a language server I've been working on for a while now, which warns the programmer (or agent) of low browser support for web features (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) right in your editor. You can also hover over almost all web features and get its support percentage and caniuse.com link.

This project is handmade, no AI was used.

Preview images & setup instructions found in the repo README. There are VS Code and Zed extensions. Language server that shows caniuse support percentages right in your editor - taylorplewe/canipls A language server that shows caniuse.com support percentages for web features, right in your editor. Implements the following features of Microsoft's Language Server Protocol : While global support percentages from canipls rarely match 1:1 those of caniuse.com's, they are never more than ~1% off , and rarely more than about 0.1% off. canipls data is refreshed once a day, but caniuse.com does not divulge all of its data sources.

hacker_news.title: Show HN: Reyn – local-first AI that journals and recalls your work
hacker_news.author: toluwajibodu
hacker_news.summary: Privacy-first desktop AI that watches your screen, builds a private journal of your work, and answers questions about your week — without sending your data to the cloud.

linked_context.page_title: Reyn — Always-On AI That Watches Your Screen, Stays on Your Mac
linked_context.page_description: Privacy-first desktop AI that watches your screen, builds a private journal of your work, and answers questions about your week — without sending your data to the cloud.
linked_context.page_excerpt:
Reyn is a privacy-first desktop AI for Mac. It watches your screen, builds a private journal of your work, and answers questions about your week — without ever sending your data to the cloud. Everything stays local on your device.

A curated network of vetted AI engineers, researchers, and domain professionals. Matched to roles at frontier AI companies and annotation programs.

hacker_news.content_context:
Hey HN, I built Reyn - which I like to describe as "granola but for everything". You're probably thinking another screen capture AI tool (which is true). Same as always, the biggest question that comes up is privacy, so I'll talk about that first

1. raw screen data is never stored in the cloud 2. user controlled filters are granular to the point that you're able to configure specific apps, windows, websites, or even keywords to be discarded immediately (once again never leaving your mac) and never captured down the pipeline

I personally built it because I find it useful and always had the problem of organizing my day (not note taking or task management), as well as sharing context on things that just happened to go undocumented throughout my day. As I was building it I decided to go even further and see if I could collect useful insights and find room for improvements in my day to day workflow.

This led to the current version of Reyn and its differentiating factor being the fact that it has a proactive layer. Most tools in this space are reactive - you ask, they retrieve. Reyn surfaces insights on its own and sends a daily recap of what you worked on, what's still open, an... Privacy-first desktop AI that watches your screen, builds a private journal of your work, and answers questions about your week — without sending your data to the cloud. Reyn is a privacy-first desktop AI for Mac. It watches your screen, builds a private journal of your work, and answers questions about your week — without ever sending your data to the cloud. Everything stays local on your d...

hacker_news.title: A framework for verifiable analysis of AI behavior
hacker_news.author: mengk
hacker_news.summary:
Developing an AI agent is a complex data analysis problem. To know if the agent is working correctly, we need to track not just benchmark scores but the details of its behavior: how do the strategies change over the course of training? Why does the new scaffold perform worse? Is there reward hacking? Answering these questions requires a combination of quantitative and qualitative analysis tailo...

Coding agents have the potential to accelerate this work, but they’re prone to subtle mistakes: they might parse data incorrectly, make unjustified assumptions, or cherry-pick examples and present a misleading narrative. These mistakes aren’t apparent in the final output. To trust a conclusion, we need to verify exactly how it was produced. But reviewing all the actions taken by a coding agent...

This problem motivated us to develop analysis plans, a framework for verifiable analysis of AI behavior. Analysis plans are specified in a Python API that any coding agent can work with. When th...

hacker_news.title: Tour the Atmosphere
hacker_news.author: doener
hacker_news.summary: Travel between clients with the browser extension, share universal Atmosphere links, and explore any account's PDS data.

linked_context.page_description: Travel between clients with the browser extension, share universal Atmosphere links, and explore any account's PDS data.
linked_context.page_excerpt:
Land on a Bluesky post and want to read it in Anisota? Click the leaf in your toolbar. The popup detects the AT URI on the page and offers every app that can render it.

Prefer not to click? Auto-redirect opens links in your preferred client. The Inspect tab surfaces the underlying record, PDS, DID, and backlinks for whatever’s on screen.

Read every collection, record, and backlink on any account’s PDS, all from your browser. Identity history, audit logs, inbound references, and a live view of network activity sit one tab away.

hacker_news.content_context:

hacker_news.title: Private Plane Crush on Texas Highway
hacker_news.author: dzonga
hacker_news.summary: Five minutes before the crash, the pilots called the Laredo airport and said they had mechanical issues, according to Laredo Police Chief Miguel Rodriguez.

linked_context.page_title: 1 dead after private plane crashes onto Texas highway, police say
linked_context.page_description: Five minutes before the crash, the pilots called the Laredo airport and said they had mechanical issues, according to Laredo Police Chief Miguel Rodriguez.
linked_context.page_excerpt:
Startup accelerator Capital Factory confirmed its CEO, Joshua Baer, was killed.

A private plane crashed late Tuesday onto a highway in Laredo, Texas, killing one of the six people on board and sending the others to the hospital, police said.

Austin-based startup accelerator Capital Factory confirmed to ABC Austin affiliate KVUE that its CEO, Joshua Baer, was killed in the plane crash.

linked_context.page_excerpt:
A minimal macOS menubar app for local, private speech-to-text dictation.

Hold a key, speak, release — your words appear wherever the cursor is. No cloud, no subscription, no data leaving your machine.

The Whisper small.en model (~150 MB) downloads automatically on first launch and is cached locally.

top_trends.title: Show HN: Canipls – see caniuse.com global support percentages as you type
top_trends.author: taylorplewe
top_trends.summary: Language server that shows caniuse support percentages right in your editor - taylorplewe/canipls

linked_context.page_title: GitHub - taylorplewe/canipls: Language server that shows caniuse support percentages right in your editor
linked_context.page_description: Language server that shows caniuse support percentages right in your editor - taylorplewe/canipls
linked_context.page_excerpt:
A language server that shows caniuse.com support percentages for web features, right in your editor.

Implements the following features of Microsoft's Language Server Protocol :

While global support percentages from canipls rarely match 1:1 those of caniuse.com's, they are never more than ~1% off , and rarely more than about 0.1% off. canipls data is refreshed once a day, but caniuse.com does not divulge all of its data sources.

top_trends.content_context:
This is a language server I've been working on for a while now, which warns the programmer (or agent) of low browser support for web features (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) right in your editor. You can also hover over almost all web features and get its support percentage and caniuse.com link.

This project is handmade, no AI was used.

Preview images & setup instructions found in the repo README. There are VS Code and Zed extensions. Language server that shows caniuse support percentages right in your editor - taylorplewe/canipls A language server that shows caniuse.com support percentages for web features, right in your editor. Implements the following features of Microsoft's Language Server Protocol : While global support percentages from canipls rarely match 1:1 those of caniuse.com's, they are never more than ~1% off , and rarely more than about 0.1% off. canipls data is refreshed once a day, but caniuse.com does not divulge all of its data sources.

top_trends.title: Show HN: Reyn – local-first AI that journals and recalls your work
top_trends.author: toluwajibodu
top_trends.summary: Privacy-first desktop AI that watches your screen, builds a private journal of your work, and answers questions about your week — without sending your data to the cloud.

linked_context.page_title: Reyn — Always-On AI That Watches Your Screen, Stays on Your Mac
linked_context.page_description: Privacy-first desktop AI that watches your screen, builds a private journal of your work, and answers questions about your week — without sending your data to the cloud.
linked_context.page_excerpt:
Reyn is a privacy-first desktop AI for Mac. It watches your screen, builds a private journal of your work, and answers questions about your week — without ever sending your data to the cloud. Everything stays local on your device.

A curated network of vetted AI engineers, researchers, and domain professionals. Matched to roles at frontier AI companies and annotation programs.

top_trends.content_context:
Hey HN, I built Reyn - which I like to describe as "granola but for everything". You're probably thinking another screen capture AI tool (which is true). Same as always, the biggest question that comes up is privacy, so I'll talk about that first

1. raw screen data is never stored in the cloud 2. user controlled filters are granular to the point that you're able to configure specific apps, windows, websites, or even keywords to be discarded immediately (once again never leaving your mac) and never captured down the pipeline

I personally built it because I find it useful and always had the problem of organizing my day (not note taking or task management), as well as sharing context on things that just happened to go undocumented throughout my day. As I was building it I decided to go even further and see if I could collect useful insights and find room for improvements in my day to day workflow.

This led to the current version of Reyn and its differentiating factor being the fact that it has a proactive layer. Most tools in this space are reactive - you ask, they retrieve. Reyn surfaces insights on its own and sends a daily recap of what you worked on, what's still open, an... Privacy-first desktop AI that watches your screen, builds a private journal of your work, and answers questions about your week — without sending your data to the cloud. Reyn is a privacy-first desktop AI for Mac. It watches your screen, builds a private journal of your work, and answers questions about your week — without ever sending your data to the cloud. Everything stays local on your d...
